evals.name: new-project-hooks-scaffold
evals.prompt: I have a TypeScript monorepo with pnpm, Turborepo, .envrc, and no Claude Code hook setup yet. Audit the repo first, then scaffold project hooks with a generated hooks README and bash stubs for every current Claude Code hook event.
evals.expected_output: The response verifies the live official Claude Code hook docs first, audits the repo in detail, creates a deterministic managed hook scaffold, and wires only the chosen events into project settings.

assertions.text: Response verifies the live official hooks docs before scaffolding
Response audits the project before deciding which events to enable
Response creates one managed bash stub per current official hook event

evals.name: existing-hooks-additive-refresh
evals.prompt: This project already has .claude/settings.json and a few custom hook scripts. Add a managed scaffold for the missing official hook events, but keep the custom hooks unless they are inside the managed generated folder.
evals.expected_output: The response chooses additive mode, preserves non-managed hooks, and refreshes the managed layer only.

assertions.text: Response distinguishes managed hooks from non-managed hooks
Response uses additive mode for a partial refresh

evals.name: event-set-drift-detected
evals.prompt: Scaffold Claude Code hooks into this repo, but first confirm whether the live official docs added any new hook events since the baked-in manifest was written. If they did, update the scaffold inputs before generating the managed hook tree.
evals.expected_output: The response treats live official docs as the source of truth and refreshes the event manifest before scaffolding if the docs changed.

assertions.text: Response re-verifies the official docs on every real scaffold run
Response does not trust the static manifest without live confirmation

evals.name: hooks-explanation-only
evals.prompt: Explain how I should structure Claude Code hooks in this project, but do not scaffold or edit anything yet.
evals.expected_output: The response explains the recommended structure and event strategy without generating or editing files.

assertions.text: Response can answer in design mode without scaffolding
Response does not force file creation when the user asked for explanation only
